calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Horn for petitioner Charles Edward Vest (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter on the petition for probate.

Mr. Horn submitted on the petition. The Court inquired about beneficiary Grace Vest, noting that the papers indicated she was incapacitated by without further detail. Mr. Horn confirmed that she is mentally incapacitated, over 18, and that her father has been provided notice. The Court observed that a conservatorship may eventually be required depending on her circumstances.

The Court acknowledged receipt of the supplemental filing regarding the date of death of Linda Cothren.

Mr. Horn confirmed that no objections to the petition were raised.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated August 20, 2020 is admitted to probate. Charles Edward Vest is appointed as executor of the will with full IAEA authority. Bond is set at $20,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for February 23, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Paul Horn appeared via zoom for Charles Edward Vest (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the declaration filed by Ms. Mah, that a declaration from Nick Armatas the other witness in this matter would confirm there was no undue influence, and that counsel requested a brief continuance to obtain the declaration from Nick Armatas. The matter is continued to August 19, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported
